info.title: Swarm User-Facing JSON-RPC API
info.description:
Single canonical user-facing API for the Swarm platform. JSON-RPC 2.0 over
HTTP for request/response, JSON-RPC subscriptions over WebSocket for live
streams. One transport, one auth model, one envelope. All operator,
builder, CLI, and dashboard consumers route through this surface.

The spec is Phase-1 in scope (internal — Empire as the only consumer at
spec time) but Phase-2-publishable by construction: no internal jargon in
method names, no [PROPOSED] placeholders that the impl never honors, all
method signatures typed, all shapes consistent across read paths. This
YAML is the OpenRPC source-of-truth — the openrpc.json artifact is
generated mechanically from method_catalog + components.

methods.name: agent.delivery_diagnostics
methods.description:
Read the owner-backed per-agent delivery diagnostics slice for failed
deliveries and terminal dead-letter deliveries. This method is a
separate owner from agent.diagnose: AgentDiagnosis remains lean and must
not expose recent failures or dead letters. The API handler validates
agent_id, list bounds, cursors, and unknown params, then consumes one
named per-agent delivery diagnostics read owner. It must not locally
reconstruct the shape from run.trace, run.diagnose, event.list,
event.get, event_receipts, dashboard helpers, CLI state, runtime memory,
or EventBus data.

Failed-delivery rows are sourced from canonical agent
event_deliveries rows with status failed. Dead-letter rows are sourced
from canonical agent event_deliveries rows with status dead_letter and
reconciled with event-scoped dead_letters records by original event id.
A terminal dead-letter delivery without at least one matching
dead_letters record is malformed owner data and must fail closed rather
than silently reporting a partial fact. Dead-letter records for events
that do not have a terminal delivery for this agent are not attributed to
this agent by this owner.

Detail rows are ordered by the canonical delivery occurrence watermark
DESC, then delivery_id DESC. The occurrence watermark is currently
COALESCE(event_deliveries.delivered_at, event_deliveries.created_at).
failure_cursor and dead_letter_cursor are independent opaque cursors
over that ordering. failures_24h and dead_letters_24h are rolling
24-hour counts over the same occurrence watermark. Detail lists return
retained rows subject to store retention and explicit per-list limits.
Empty lists are represented as empty arrays.

params.name: failure_limit
params.description: Max failed-delivery detail rows to return.

params.name: failure_cursor
params.description: Opaque cursor returned by failures_next_cursor for the next failed-delivery page.

params.name: dead_letter_limit
params.description: Max terminal dead-letter delivery detail rows to return.

params.name: dead_letter_cursor
params.description: Opaque cursor returned by dead_letters_next_cursor for the next terminal dead-letter delivery page.

methods.name: agent.delivery_lifecycle
methods.description:
Read the owner-backed per-agent delivery lifecycle rows across the
canonical event_deliveries lifecycle states. This method is the
canonical agent-scoped lifecycle read owner for pending, in_progress,
delivered, failed, and dead_letter delivery rows. It is a separate
owner from agent.diagnose and agent.delivery_diagnostics: AgentDiagnosis
remains lean, and rich failed/dead-letter evidence remains owned by
agent.delivery_diagnostics.

The API handler validates agent_id, optional run_id, optional
delivery_status filters, list bounds, cursor, and unknown params, then
consumes one named persisted delivery lifecycle read owner. The owner
reads only canonical event_deliveries rows for subscriber_type agent
joined to the minimum canonical event identity fields needed for
display: event_id, event_name, run_id, and entity_id. It must not
reconstruct lifecycle rows from run.trace, event.get, event.list,
agent.diagnose, agent.delivery_diagnostics, event_receipts,
dead_letters, logs, dashboard/Builder helpers, CLI state, EventBus
memory, runtime memory, or direct DB/store readers outside this owner.

Rows are ordered by event_deliveries.created_at DESC, then delivery_id
DESC. cursor is an opaque cursor over that ordering. run_id filters use
the canonical delivery/event run identity, currently
COALESCE(event_deliveries.run_id, events.run_id), and return only rows
matching the supplied run. delivery_status filters are exact
event_deliveries.status values. Empty lists are represented as empty
arrays. The method reports only lifecycle summaries
(status/timestamps/retry_count/reason_code/failure); it does not
include dead-letter records, route target details, retry-policy
recommendations, status hints, trace reconstruction, event rendering,
or CLI/dashboard presentation semantics.

params.name: run_id
params.description: Optional run filter over COALESCE(event_deliveries.run_id, events.run_id).

params.name: delivery_status
params.description: Optional exact lifecycle-status filter. When supplied, only these canonical event_deliveries.status values are returned.

params.name: limit
params.description: Max lifecycle rows to return.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Opaque cursor returned by next_cursor for the next lifecycle page.

methods.name: agent.diagnose
methods.description:
Read the owner-backed diagnosis slice for one agent. This method is
limited to agent identity/status refs, current/last conversation refs,
pending-delivery queue summary/detail evidence, and optional delivery
lifecycle evidence. It may also return runtime_state.watchdog as a
narrow projection of the canonical active session watchdog descriptor,
active as selected active-session latest-turn refs, and
last_tool_outcome as compact selected active-session latest-turn
canonical tool-result evidence. active is omitted when no active live
session is selected or the selected session has no latest turn.
active.turn_id is required when active is present; active.task_id and
active.entity_id are omitted when empty or absent. active.entity_id is
the latest selected agent_turns.entity_id dispatch entity and must not
fall back to the agent configured/effective entity, task target entity,
role-scoped current-entity tool context, dashboard projection,
conversation API response, or stale session evidence.
last_tool_outcome is omitted when no active live session is selected,
no latest turn exists, no canonical turn summary exists, or the
canonical summary has no tool result. last_tool_outcome.ok is the
selected turn parse_ok value and is not a per-tool execution status;
richer per-tool status, latency, completed_at, and error diagnostics
remain split.
runtime_state.watchdog is omitted when no active live session or no
active-session watchdog evidence exists, and malformed watchdog data
fails closed. Queue details are paged with queue_limit and
queue_cursor, ordered by events.created_at ASC then events.event_id ASC.
AgentPendingDelivery.enqueued_at uses the same timestamp owner as
oldest_pending_age_seconds. AgentPendingDelivery.attempts is the
canonical recorded retry counter from event_deliveries.retry_count, with
no API-layer +1 or status arithmetic. The API handler consumes the
canonical agent diagnosis read owner directly, including its
watchdog-backed runtime_state sub-owner, selected-turn active sub-owner,
and selected-turn last_tool_outcome sub-owner. It must not locally join
conversation, run, trace, token, CLI, or dashboard owners, and it must
not expose the full runtime_state
enum, top-level watchdog, run_id, bundle_version, actual in-flight
active work, configured/effective current entity, task target entity,
role-scoped current entity, token usage, recent failures, or dead
letters.

params.name: queue_limit
params.description: Max pending-delivery detail rows to return. pending_count remains the total pending count for the selected queue, not the page length.

params.name: queue_cursor
params.description: Opaque cursor returned by queue.next_cursor for the next pending-delivery detail page.

methods.name: agent.get
methods.description:
Read one agent's identity, current operational status, and references
to its current session and last turn. Returns refs only — full session
and transcript content is read via conversation.* methods. Ownership
boundary: agent.* never returns transcripts.

methods.name: agent.list
methods.description: List declared agents in the loaded bundle, with current operational status.

methods.name: agent.replay
methods.description:
Agent-scoped projection of the canonical operator-facing event redelivery primitive.

Redelivers one existing persisted event to exactly one requested original agent subscriber. This method MUST consume the same canonical event redelivery owner as event.replay by projecting the request to the singleton subscriber set [agent_id]. It MUST NOT implement independent replay target derivation, fresh replay event creation, audit emission, idempotency, or delivery/session interpretation.

The target authority remains the original event_deliveries agent rows for event_id. If agent_id was not an original agent subscriber for that event, the method fails closed with EVENT_REPLAY_SUBSCRIBER_NOT_ORIGINAL. Current live subscriptions, route tables, bundle declarations, or agent backlog state must not add recipients.

The replay preserves the original event, delivery, receipt, and session inspection surface, creates a distinct replay event identity with source_event_id/parent lineage to the original event, creates a fresh delivery row for the selected agent, and returns the single original/new delivery projection. The synthetic audit event is the canonical event.replayed event; this method does not create agent.event_replayed or mutate/abort the original session.

methods.name: agent.replay_backlog
methods.description: Replay the agent's pending event backlog. Used for recovery after restart or ingress pause.

methods.name: agent.restart
methods.description: Restart an agent's session. Existing in-flight turn is killed; agent re-initializes.

methods.name: agent.send_directive
methods.description: Send a directive (free-form instruction) to a running agent. The runtime resolves the target run before reservation: explicit run_id targets an existing nonterminal run; with no run_id, zero active sessions allocate a new run_id, one active session uses that session's run_id, and multiple active sessions fail closed with AMBIGUOUS_RUN_TARGET. One agent_directive_operations row and one platform.agent_directive event are persisted atomically before the operation receives its sole BoardStep admission. Successful BoardStep response is made durable before pipeline settlement and API completion are finalized. Same-key retries consume the same operation and never re-execute an admitted, executed, failed, or indeterminate operation. Keyless calls create fresh operations and make no cross-call exactly-once claim.

params.name: run_id
params.description: Optional explicit nonterminal run target. If omitted, the runtime resolves from active sessions or allocates a new run_id.

response.description: Optional acknowledgement or error from the agent.

active_sessions.description: Active sessions that prevent implicit target resolution. A session without run_id is reported without a run_id field and still fail-closes implicit resolution.

run_id.description: Present when ambiguity was caused by an explicit run target combined with incompatible legacy targeting flags.

details.description: A matching directive operation owns a live execution lease. Retry may observe its durable outcome but must not execute it again.
Directive execution succeeded and is durable, but settlement/API completion still requires repair. Retry performs finalization only.
The admitted directive execution returned a durable terminal failure. Retrying the operation never executes BoardStep again.
Directive execution may have produced effects, but no authoritative result was durably recorded. Automatic re-execution is forbidden.

methods.name: agent.usage
methods.description:
Read the owner-backed per-agent LLM token and estimated-cost usage
aggregate. This is a separate owner from agent.diagnose:
AgentDiagnosis remains lean and must not expose token usage. The API
handler validates agent_id and optional created_at window params, then
consumes one named per-agent usage read owner over spend_ledger facts.
It must not locally reconstruct usage from raw request_payload,
response_payload, agent.diagnose, agent.delivery_diagnostics,
run.trace, run.diagnose, dashboard helpers, CLI state, EventBus data,
or runtime memory.

The owner is agent-scoped and aggregates retained spend_ledger rows by
spend_ledger.created_at. since is inclusive; until is exclusive. If no
window is supplied, all retained rows for the agent are aggregated. The
response separates exact and estimated usage_accounting totals and
provides model alias, backend profile, provider, transport, and concrete
model breakdowns so provider usage and estimated CLI usage are not
silently merged. estimated_cost_usd is the
BudgetTracker guardrail cost estimate, not provider billing truth. This
method does not make run_id, session_id, turn_id, last-turn, or
provider-billing claims.

params.name: since
params.description: Inclusive lower bound over spend_ledger.created_at.

params.name: until
params.description: Exclusive upper bound over spend_ledger.created_at; must be after since when both are supplied.

methods.name: bundle.agents
methods.description: Return definition-only agent catalog entries projected from the persisted bundle row identified by bundle_hash. This method must not read runtime agents, sessions, queues, diagnoses, active tasks, or status fields, and does not close strict duplicate-slug runtime identity work.

methods.name: bundle.delete
methods.description: Destructive bundle delete wrapper. The API layer owns only the v1 request/response/auth/idempotency contract and MUST consume the bundle-delete owner chain for execution. Omitted or false force performs the #1018 non-force path: plan persisted run users, fail closed with BUNDLE_HAS_ACTIVE_RUNS when running or paused persisted users exist, and otherwise consume the #1009 source-first final store transaction to mark eligible persisted runs deleted before deleting the bundle row. force=true performs the #1019 force path: shared destructive-operation mutex, bundle plan, preservation cleanup for active affected runs, managed entity-container selection/stop by run identity, and the same final store transaction after cleanup succeeds. This method MUST NOT implement CLI behavior, runtime.nuke include_bundles, or broad multi-bundle lifecycle cleanup.

methods.name: bundle.get
methods.description: Read one persisted bundle catalog row by canonical bundle_hash. The result returns stored config, parsed projection, metadata, and data presence facts only; it does not expose runtime state or server-local filesystem paths.

methods.name: bundle.list
methods.description: List persisted bundle catalog rows. This is a definition/catalog read over the bundles store owner, not runtime state. Results are ordered by newest ingested_at then bundle_hash and may be cursor-paginated.

methods.name: bundle.register
methods.description: Register a bundle into the persisted bundle catalog without starting a runtime. The request submits a BundleRegistrationEnvelopeV1 YAML document plus optional BundleRegisterDataBlobV1 entries. The server materializes those files into a temporary contract root, computes the canonical bundle_hash through internal/runtime/contracts.BundleHash, stores canonical bundle catalog projection bytes through the shared store UPSERT owner, and returns the canonical bundle_hash. This method never mutates runs and never restores rows whose bundle_source was previously marked deleted.

params.name: content_yaml
params.description: YAML-encoded BundleRegistrationEnvelopeV1. This is an upload envelope, not server-local filesystem authority.

methods.name: conversation.fork
methods.description:
Create a forkchat lifecycle session from an existing conversation source
session and a validated fork point. This first slice is lifecycle-only:
it persists source session/run/agent lineage, fork point descriptor,
creator token, fixed 24-hour TTL, and deletion state. It does not
execute a forked LLM turn, return entity snapshots, expose inherited
transcript counts, or write into normal conversation/event/log surfaces.

methods.name: conversation.fork_chat
methods.description:
Execute one sandboxed forkchat turn against an active fork lifecycle session.
The method consumes conversation_forks for lifecycle validation,
conversation_fork_snapshots for source-at-fork context, and
conversation_fork_turns for fork-local transcript persistence. It does
execute the source agent through the forkchat sandbox executor, provides
fork-snapshot read tools, and records only sandbox-observed requested
side-effecting tools as stubbed fork-local results under
ConversationForkSandboxPolicy. The forkchat executor owns the synthetic
actor and resolves it through the canonical regular model alias so every
admitted provider backend receives an explicit model selection. The
executor classifies provider execution as operator infrastructure rather
than a managed-agent lifecycle generation; it MUST NOT fabricate a live
agent lifecycle token. It does not write agent_turns, events,
runtime logs, mailbox rows, run commands, or dashboard conversation rows
by default.

methods.name: conversation.fork_delete
methods.description: Soft-delete one forkchat lifecycle session from the isolated conversation_forks owner.

methods.name: conversation.fork_list
methods.description: List active forkchat lifecycle sessions from the isolated conversation_forks owner.

methods.name: conversation.fork_view
methods.description: Read one forkchat lifecycle session header/state and fork-local transcript turns owned by conversation_fork_turns.

methods.name: conversation.get_turn
methods.description: Read one closed author-safe turn projection by exact canonical full turn ID within a conversation. Raw provider payloads, private reasoning, unmarked progress, tool payloads, capability telemetry, and automatic runtime-log joins are not part of this contract.

methods.name: conversation.list
methods.description: List conversation summaries (one per session).

methods.name: conversation.list_turns
methods.description: List compact closed author-safe turn rows for one conversation. Pages are newest first, bounded to 500 rows, and use an opaque cursor over the canonical persisted created_at plus turn_id order. Rows contain owner-derived activity counts and never contain ordered activity or author-visible text. Ordinal is a stable display projection and is never an input identity.

methods.name: entity.aggregate
methods.description: Aggregate entity counts grouped by a field.

params.name: group_by
params.description: Field to group by. Defaults to current_state when omitted.

methods.name: entity.get
methods.description: Read one entity in full, including fields, gates, and accumulator state.

params.name: run_id
params.description: Disambiguates entities reused across runs.

methods.name: entity.list
methods.description: List entities with optional filters. Consolidates legacy "instance" and "entity" reads.

methods.name: event.get
methods.description: Read one event with its full delivery and dead-letter detail.

methods.name: event.list
methods.description:
Canonical event store query. Folds dead-letter inspection in via the
has_dead_letter and reason_code filters; no separate dead_letter.*
namespace in v1. Multi-bundle-safe reads require explicit filter.run_id; unscoped event firehose reads are not a public v1 surface.

methods.name: event.publish
methods.description:
Canonical low-level event publication and workflow-creation primitive.
Publishes one declared event into the runtime bus. If run_id is omitted,
the server allocates a new run_id, persists the event as that run's
trigger, and returns new_run_created=true. If run_id is provided,
it must identify an existing nonterminal run; missing or terminal
runs fail closed with RUN_NOT_FOUND or RUN_ALREADY_TERMINAL. Existing-run publication must preflight the selected runtime EventBus recipient plan before event/idempotency persistence. A declared event with no selected persisted delivery recipient or delivery route for that run fails closed with EVENT_NOT_DECLARED and must not return success with deliveries=0. Target resolution failures in the selected plan also fail closed with EVENT_NOT_DECLARED. Existing-run publication may provide `target` as the singular receiver route identity (`flow_instance` plus `entity_id`). When present, `target` is route authority and the server validates that the entity exists in the selected run, belongs to the selected target flow instance, and has a selected delivery route for the requested event before event/idempotency persistence. The API must not infer an entity target from run_id or payload.entity_id.

Checkpoint/source-authority policy: callers that need causal proof
MUST provide source_event_id together with run_id. The source event
must exist in the same run_id and the server persists it as
events.source_event_id / ParentEventID. Omitted source_event_id
means the published event is unparented; it is valid low-level
ingress but MUST NOT be presented as proof that an internal
approval/checkpoint path occurred. source_event_id without run_id,
malformed source_event_id, or a source event from another run fails
closed before event or idempotency completion persistence. A missing
source event fails closed with EVENT_NOT_FOUND.

The method consumes the same canonical EventBus/store event-publication
owner used by runtime dispatch. Delivery results are read back from the
persisted event_deliveries owner; the API layer must not re-plan
subscribers. A successful event.publish response is a durable acceptance acknowledgment for the event record, selected delivery manifest, and committed replay scope; it MUST NOT wait for system-node handler completion, dynamic auto_emit_on_create side effects, agent processing, downstream event delivery, or downstream receipts. Delivery statuses in the response are a persisted snapshot and may remain pending. Post-commit runtime dispatch/recovery owns downstream completion. This is the canonical primitive; run.start remains only
as a deprecated wrapper for older callers.

`event_name` is a contract event reference. Callers may pass a root/unambiguous leaf event name or a flow-scoped reference such as `flow_id/local_event` or `flow-path/local-event`. The server resolves flow-scoped references through the loaded contract catalog before declaration validation, payload validation, EventBus publish, and idempotency completion persistence. Unknown flow/event combinations and ambiguous unscoped leaf names fail closed with EVENT_NOT_DECLARED and no mutation.

Create-entity ownership: handler create_entity is the only authority that materializes the canonical receiving-flow entity identity. Caller-supplied payload.entity_id on an event whose selected handler declares create_entity fails closed with PAYLOAD_VALIDATION_FAILED before event/idempotency persistence. select_entity/select_or_create_entity use their declared payload-key bindings only; payload.entity_id is not target or stateful-context authority.

params.name: bundle_hash
params.description: Canonical #1001 bundle identity scope. Must be bundle-v1:sha256:<64 lowercase hex>. Required when run_id is omitted. When run_id identifies an existing run, optional bundle_hash must match that run's canonical runs.bundle_hash or the method fails closed with BUNDLE_MISMATCH. In DB-loaded RuntimeContextManager mode, the resolved hash must select a loaded BundleContext or the method fails closed with BUNDLE_UNAVAILABLE. cannot be combined with legacy bundle_ref; malformed or combined inputs fail closed with UNSUPPORTED_BUNDLE_HASH.

params.name: bundle_ref
params.description: Deprecated transition alias during #1001. Carries bundle_ref.fingerprint only, is not authoritative for create-new-work scope or routing, and cannot be combined with bundle_hash.

params.name: event_name
params.description: Contract event reference to publish. Root and unambiguous leaf names remain accepted; valid flow_id/local_event or flow-path/local-event references are resolved server-side through the loaded contract catalog to the canonical runtime event identity before validation and persistence. Unknown flow/event combinations and ambiguous unscoped leaf names fail closed with EVENT_NOT_DECLARED.

params.name: payload
params.description: JSON object business payload. payload.entity_id is not an event envelope, target, or stateful-context authority. If the selected handler uses create_entity, caller-supplied payload.entity_id fails closed with PAYLOAD_VALIDATION_FAILED because the handler materializes the platform-owned canonical receiving-flow identity.

params.name: run_id
params.description: Existing nonterminal run to inject into; if omitted, runtime allocates and starts a new run.

params.name: source_event_id
params.description: Optional parent/source event id for explicit checkpoint lineage. Requires run_id and must identify an event in the same run.

params.name: target
params.description: Optional singular receiver route identity for existing-run publication. Requires run_id and carries the concrete target flow_instance plus entity_id that the server persists as event.target before EventBus recipient planning. This field is route authority; payload.entity_id and run_id are not target authority. Fan-out target_set and selector-style target forms are not part of the v1 event.publish public surface in this slice.

params.name: emitter
params.description: Optional producer identifier. Defaults to cli-publish:<actor_token_id>.

details.description: The requested event is not admitted by the selected event catalog, root-input contract, target, or recipient plan. Root-input reasons preserve the canonical declared and routable domains.

declared_events.description: Deterministically sorted event domain from the owning server validator. For root-input reasons this is the root flow pins.inputs.events declaration.

reason.description: Named server rejection branch. Root-input values are not_declared_root_input and declared_root_input_not_routable.

routable_events.description: Deterministically sorted root inputs with a derived runtime route. Present for root-input reasons, including an empty array when none are routable.

methods.name: event.replay
methods.description:
Canonical operator-facing event redelivery primitive.

Redelivers one existing persisted event to all original agent subscribers, or to a caller-supplied subset of those original agent subscribers. The target set is owned by the original event_deliveries agent rows; current live subscriptions, route tables, and bundle declarations must not add recipients. Requested subscribers outside the original agent delivery set fail closed with EVENT_REPLAY_SUBSCRIBER_NOT_ORIGINAL.

Public replay creates fresh replay execution evidence. Implementations MUST preserve the original event, delivery, receipt, and session inspection surface, and MUST create a distinct replay event identity whose source_event_id/parent lineage points at the original event. The fresh replay event creates fresh event_deliveries rows, and the result reports source_delivery_id lineage back to the original delivery rows.

Only terminal original delivery rows are replayable in v1: delivered, failed, and dead_letter. Pending or in_progress originals fail closed with EVENT_REPLAY_NOT_ELIGIBLE. If the original event has no replayable original agent delivery history, the method fails with EVENT_REPLAY_NO_DELIVERY_HISTORY.

The server also persists a synthetic event.replayed audit event with original event identity, replay event identity, selected subscribers, actor token identity, and original delivery summary. event.list, event.get, run.trace, and event.subscribe observe this audit event through the normal event read/stream owners.

Runtime pause and run-dispatch blocking follow the queueable event-producing ingress rule: replay/audit events and delivery rows may be persisted, but subscriber work must not dispatch until resume/unblock. Missing or unregistered selected original subscribers fail closed before replay evidence is created with EVENT_REPLAY_SUBSCRIBER_UNAVAILABLE.

params.name: subscribers
params.description: Optional subset of original agent subscriber IDs. Empty or omitted means all original agent subscribers.

methods.name: event.subscribe
methods.description:
Live event stream with the same filter shape as event.list. Filter
parity is required by the conventions section. Pagination params
(limit, cursor) and the time-window `until` are NOT accepted here
— subscriptions deliver everything matching the filter live. Multi-bundle-safe streams require
explicit filter.run_id; unscoped event firehose subscriptions are not a public v1 surface.

params.name: replay_since
params.description: Optional catch-up window. If present, delivers events from this time forward, then continues live. Subject to runtime event retention.

methods.name: health.check
methods.description: Structured health and bundle identity. Operators use this to confirm readiness and the served bundle identity; runtime-instance descriptor trust uses runtime.identity.

methods.name: health.ping
methods.description: Cheap aliveness check. Always returns success when the runtime is responsive.

methods.name: health.subscribe
methods.description: WebSocket heartbeat subscription. Notifications carry the same shape as health.check returns.

methods.name: mailbox.acknowledge
methods.description: Acknowledge a notice. Decision cards reject this operation with a teaching error.

methods.name: mailbox.begin_input
methods.description: Start a durable actor/card/verdict-scoped input draft. This operation never creates a verdict.

methods.name: mailbox.cancel_input
methods.description: Cancel the caller's still-active durable input draft. Foreign, stale, expired, replaced, or consumed drafts fail closed.

methods.name: mailbox.decide
methods.description: Commit one authored verdict against a pending typed decision card. The registered anchor authority, typed verdict/fields, change cursor, mailbox.card_decided event, and idempotency response commit atomically. Outcome routing consumes the frozen card snapshot after commit.

methods.name: mailbox.defer
methods.description: Defer a pending typed decision card. Defer is non-terminal: status remains pending and only deferred_until changes. It never chooses a verdict or routes the stage. The admitted decision time and until value use the decision-card UTC-microsecond timestamp contract.

methods.name: mailbox.get
methods.description: Read one mailbox item with full payload, decision history, and the server-owned decision sheet used by CLI/operator review surfaces.

methods.name: mailbox.list
methods.description: List mailbox items. Filters cover the operator workflow of "find items requiring my attention."

methods.name: mailbox.subscribe
methods.description: Resumable at-least-once decision-card change feed. Reconnect requires mailbox.list reconciliation.

methods.name: rpc.unsubscribe
methods.description: Cancel an active WebSocket subscription on the current connection. This is a JSON-RPC protocol method for the subscription transport, not a durable resource mutation.

methods.name: run.continue
methods.description: Resume a paused run.

methods.name: run.diagnose
methods.description:
Interpreted run diagnosis. Returns the run header plus the
operator-state projection from
internal/store/run_operational_status_read_surface.go and concrete failed-delivery evidence from
RunDebugReport.FailedDeliveries, plus result.test_quiescence from
RunDebugReport.TestQuiescence for deterministic test assertion barriers. The single canonical owner for
"why isn't this run progressing?" routes persisted evidence through
the API instead of having every consumer recompute it.

methods.name: run.fork
methods.description:
Public selected-contract run fork mutation.

Creates and executes a selected-contract fork from a source run. The method consumes source bundle availability from runs.bundle_source / runs.bundle_hash and bundle-row presence before any fork mutation. Source runs whose canonical bundle bytes are unavailable fail closed with BUNDLE_UNAVAILABLE; persisted source rows with missing bundle data fail with BUNDLE_DATA_INTEGRITY_ERROR. The selected-contract execution path consumes the existing store/runtime run_fork selected_contracts owners and must not infer source identity from legacy bundle_fingerprint, bundle_ref, null-collapse, or local filesystem heuristics.

If bundle_hash is omitted, the source run's canonical bundle_hash is used. If bundle_hash is supplied and equals the source run bundle_hash, the same-bundle selected_contracts fork may proceed. Disk-loaded source execution uses the existing runtime selected-contract source loader. DB-loaded same-bundle source execution must load source bytes from the persisted bundle catalog runtime source owner, verify the reconstructed bundle_hash, and stamp fork-created runs with bundle_source=persisted plus that canonical bundle_hash.

If bundle_hash differs from the source run bundle_hash, the target hash selects an already loaded/boot-pinned RuntimeContextManager BundleContext under current Option A. The method must persist selected-contract ownership as mode=bundle_hash with that target bundle_hash, recover routes against the same target identity, execute against the target BundleContext runtime/source, and stamp fork-created runs with bundle_source=persisted plus the target canonical bundle_hash. Valid target hashes that are not loaded by this process, have been unloaded or deactivated, are missing/deleted from the catalog, or otherwise lack supported persisted bytes fail closed with BUNDLE_UNAVAILABLE. Corrupt or hash-mismatched persisted target bytes fail with BUNDLE_DATA_INTEGRITY_ERROR. run.fork MUST NOT perform Dynamic Load, bundle.register runtime start, catalog-only runtime load, ambient CLI bundle selection, or Option B artifact capture. The `swarm run fork` CLI consumes this method and must not call runtime/store helpers directly.

params.name: source_run_id
params.description: Source run to fork from.

params.name: fork_event_id
params.description: Optional explicit fork-point event id in source_run_id. When omitted, the run-fork store owner selects the latest persisted source-run event; no CLI/current-session ambient event is allowed.

params.name: bundle_hash
params.description: Optional target/source bundle selector. Equal or omitted values use same-bundle selected_contracts semantics. A differing value selects an already loaded/boot-pinned RuntimeContextManager BundleContext under #976; valid but unavailable targets fail with BUNDLE_UNAVAILABLE and corrupt target catalog bytes fail with BUNDLE_DATA_INTEGRITY_ERROR.

methods.name: run.get
methods.description: Cheap canonical read of the run header. Used by lists, hovers, and any consumer that needs run status without paying for diagnostic interpretation.

methods.name: run.list
methods.description: List runs with optional filters. Cursor-paginated. The optional bundle_hash filter is a canonical persisted run-bundle selector over runs.bundle_hash; callers must not use legacy bundle fingerprints for this field.

params.name: bundle_hash
params.description: Optional canonical bundle selector over runs.bundle_hash. Must be bundle-v1:sha256:<64 lowercase hex>.

methods.name: run.pause
methods.description: Pause a run by halting event dispatch. In-flight handlers complete; queued events wait. Run continues on run.continue.

methods.name: run.start
methods.description:
Deprecated wrapper over event.publish retained for older Builder/API callers. New clients should call event.publish directly.
Start a run by injecting an event into the runtime bus. Create-new-work admission follows event.publish bundle_hash semantics: if no existing run row provides bundle context, bundle_hash is required. If run_id is supplied but no run row exists, the call is still create-new-work and run_id alone is not bundle scope. If caller bundle_hash and existing run context disagree, the method fails closed with BUNDLE_MISMATCH. In DB-loaded RuntimeContextManager mode, the resolved bundle_hash must select a loaded BundleContext or the method fails closed with BUNDLE_UNAVAILABLE. Legacy bundle_ref.fingerprint is deprecated transition input only and cannot be combined with bundle_hash.

params.name: bundle_hash
params.description: Canonical #1001 bundle identity scope. Must be bundle-v1:sha256:<64 lowercase hex>. Required when no existing run row supplies bundle context. When run_id identifies an existing run, optional bundle_hash must match that run's canonical runs.bundle_hash or the method fails closed with BUNDLE_MISMATCH. In DB-loaded RuntimeContextManager mode, the resolved hash must select a loaded BundleContext or the method fails closed with BUNDLE_UNAVAILABLE; cannot be combined with legacy bundle_ref; malformed or combined inputs fail closed with UNSUPPORTED_BUNDLE_HASH.

params.name: bundle_ref
params.description: Deprecated transition alias during #1001. Carries bundle_ref.fingerprint only, is not authoritative for create-new-work scope or routing, and cannot be combined with bundle_hash.

params.name: run_id
params.description: Caller-provided ID; if omitted, runtime assigns.

details.description: The requested event is not admitted by the selected event catalog, root-input contract, target, or recipient plan. Root-input reasons preserve the canonical declared and routable domains.

declared_events.description: Deterministically sorted event domain from the owning server validator. For root-input reasons this is the root flow pins.inputs.events declaration.

reason.description: Named server rejection branch. Root-input values are not_declared_root_input and declared_root_input_not_routable.

routable_events.description: Deterministically sorted root inputs with a derived runtime route. Present for root-input reasons, including an empty array when none are routable.

methods.name: run.stop
methods.description: Stop an in-progress run. Pending events are abandoned; in-flight handlers complete or roll back per their own semantics. The selected run-control store terminalizes pending same-run `event_deliveries` rows for canonical runtime subscriber types (`agent` and `node`) as `dead_letter` with `reason_code=run_stopped`, and writes the matching agent or node receipt. For events that already have a platform/pipeline receipt, run.stop preserves that receipt as the authoritative routing outcome while terminalizing the pending delivery. When a stopped event lacks a platform/pipeline receipt and no active delivery remains, the store writes a stopped-run pipeline dead-letter so missing-pipeline-receipt replay cannot resurrect abandoned work.

methods.name: run.subscribe_trace
methods.description:
Live composed trace stream for one run. Each notification carries one
joined trace row (the same RunTraceRow shape returned by run.trace).
Distinct from event.subscribe because trace is a composed view (event
+ delivery + session + turn joined by the runtime), not raw events.
Supports the same RunTraceFilter row predicates as run.trace, composed with
replay_since for catch-up/recovery.

params.name: filter
params.description: Typed predicates over canonical RunTraceRow facts. Present fields are OR-lists within the field and AND-composed across fields.

params.name: include_internal
params.description: Optional trace visibility control. Defaults to false. When omitted or false, run.subscribe_trace excludes `platform.runtime_log` rows; when true, those internal runtime-log trace rows are included in notifications.

params.name: replay_since
params.description: Optional catch-up window. If present, delivers trace rows from this time forward, then continues live. Subject to runtime trace retention.

methods.name: run.trace
methods.description:
Persisted composed run trace as an ordered list of joined rows. Each
row is anchored on an event and carries delivery, session, and turn
fields where the join finds them. The runtime owns the join — clients
MUST consume rows as-emitted and MUST NOT reconstruct the join from
separate event/delivery/session/turn lists. Backed by the canonical
RunDebugTraceRow surface in internal/store/run_debug_read_surface.go.

params.name: since
params.description: Optional RFC3339/RFC3339Nano materialization watermark. Returns rows whose canonical trace materialization watermark is strictly after this timestamp.

params.name: until
params.description: Optional snapshot-only RFC3339/RFC3339Nano materialization watermark upper bound. Returns rows whose canonical trace materialization watermark is less than or equal to this timestamp. When combined with since, the server applies the half-open window `(since, until]`; since after until is invalid.

params.name: filter
params.description: Typed predicates over canonical RunTraceRow facts. Present fields are OR-lists within the field and AND-composed across fields.

params.name: include_internal
params.description: Optional trace visibility control. Defaults to false. When omitted or false, run.trace excludes `platform.runtime_log` rows; when true, those internal runtime-log trace rows are included.

methods.name: runtime.identity
methods.description: Authenticated nonsensitive identity for the live runtime process instance. Local context descriptors use this method to prove that a discovered API endpoint is the same runtime instance named by the descriptor. It is not bundle identity, PID, port, URL, /healthz, or /readyz.

methods.name: runtime.incidents
methods.description: Aggregated runtime incidents (errors and warnings grouped by error_code/component for triage). The optional bundle_hash filter is applied to source runtime-log rows before aggregation.

params.name: bundle_hash
params.description: Optional canonical bundle selector over runs.bundle_hash. Must be bundle-v1:sha256:<64 lowercase hex>.

methods.name: runtime.logs
methods.description: Filtered structured runtime log query. The optional bundle_hash filter is a canonical persisted run-bundle selector over runs.bundle_hash and composes with run_id and other filters as AND.

params.name: bundle_hash
params.description: Optional canonical bundle selector over runs.bundle_hash. Must be bundle-v1:sha256:<64 lowercase hex>.

methods.name: runtime.nuke
methods.description:
Public destructive runtime reset wrapper. The API layer owns only the v1 request/response/auth/idempotency contract and MUST consume the destructive-reset owner chain for execution: plan/result/lock, active-run and delivery quiescence, run-scoped cleanup/preservation, and managed entity-container selection/stop. It MUST NOT directly truncate tables, call raw Docker stop/list helpers, call AgentManager.ResetRuntimeStateWithSource, or migrate dashboard/Builder/CLI reset seams in this method implementation.

`dry_run=true` returns the same top-level shape with side-effect-free previews from each owner. Apply mode sequences plan -> quiescence -> cleanup -> managed-container stop. Managed-container stop failures are represented as `status=partial_failure`, `ok=false`, and explicit `errors`; they must not masquerade as complete success. `include_bundles` defaults true and is the only runtime.nuke control for whether server-wide bundle catalog state is deleted with the cleanup owner; false preserves bundle catalog rows. In DB-loaded RuntimeContextManager mode, successful or partial apply-mode runtime.nuke requests with `include_bundles=true` also mark loaded BundleContext entries unavailable/unloaded so later supported admissions fail closed with BUNDLE_UNAVAILABLE; `include_bundles=false` must not deactivate loaded contexts. Public final-response idempotency follows the v1 API idempotency convention.

methods.name: runtime.pause
methods.description: Pause runtime ingress. Queueable event-producing ingress is persisted but not dispatched until runtime.resume; synchronous MCP/tool request-response ingress fails closed while paused. In-flight handlers complete.

methods.name: runtime.resume
methods.description: Resume runtime ingress. Queued event-producing ingress becomes eligible for normal EventBus dispatch exactly once through canonical delivery/idempotency owners.

methods.name: runtime.subscribe_logs
methods.description:
Live structured runtime log stream for `runtime.logs` follow mode.
Notifications carry one `LogEntry` row from the same persisted runtime log read owner used by `runtime.logs`.
The method accepts the same runtime-log identity/content filter fields as `runtime.logs`, but snapshot pagination, sort, and fixed-window controls (`limit`, `cursor`, `order`, `since`, `until`) are not accepted on the subscription. Use `replay_since` for catch-up.

params.name: bundle_hash
params.description: Optional canonical bundle selector over runs.bundle_hash. Must be bundle-v1:sha256:<64 lowercase hex>.

params.name: replay_since
params.description: Optional catch-up window. If present, delivers runtime log entries from this time forward, then continues live. Subject to runtime log retention.

methods.name: test.setup_entities
methods.description: Public deterministic scenario-runner setup/pre-state mutation helper. This method seeds scenario-owned initial entity rows for exactly one scenario run after the runner has validated the authored setup against the loaded contract bundle. It is not a general product/runtime entity.create or entity.update surface, does not publish events, does not create delivery rows, does not infer handler outcomes, and does not accept private cataloge2e setup syntax. The server persists the run row under canonical bundle context and writes the requested initial entity state through the store owner. Scenario-visible verification remains through event.publish, run.diagnose/run.trace, entity.get/list, and other public read owners.

params.name: bundle_hash
params.description: Canonical bundle identity for the loaded contract package. Legacy bundle_ref/fingerprint inputs are not accepted.

params.name: run_id
params.description: Deterministic scenario run id generated by the public runner before the first action step.

params.name: entities
params.description: Validated scenario setup entity rows. Alias and entity_id must be unique within the request.

flow_instance.description: Concrete flow scope for the setup entity; omitted for root-flow setup rows.

dead_letter_records.description: Event-scoped dead-letter records reconciled to this terminal agent delivery through the original event id. A terminal dead-letter delivery without at least one matching record is malformed owner data and fails closed.

occurred_at.description: Canonical delivery occurrence watermark, currently COALESCE(event_deliveries.delivered_at, event_deliveries.created_at).

status.description: Terminal dead-letter delivery subfact from the canonical event_deliveries delivery status owner.

dead_letters.description: Recent retained terminal dead-letter delivery rows for this agent, ordered by occurred_at DESC then delivery_id DESC.

failures.description: Recent retained failed delivery rows for this agent, ordered by occurred_at DESC then delivery_id DESC.

dead_letters_24h.description: Rolling 24-hour count of terminal dead-letter agent delivery rows using the same occurrence watermark as AgentDeadLetterDelivery.occurred_at.

failures_24h.description: Rolling 24-hour count of failed agent delivery rows using the same occurrence watermark as AgentDeliveryFailure.occurred_at.

occurred_at.description: Canonical delivery occurrence watermark, currently COALESCE(event_deliveries.delivered_at, event_deliveries.created_at).

status.description: Failed-delivery subfact from the canonical event_deliveries delivery status owner.

blocking_layer.description: Current producer owner is internal/store/agent_lifecycle_read_surface.go agentLifecycleBlockingLayer, consuming deliverylifecycle.StateFromDelivery. Current reachable state/layer pairs are queued/delivery_queue, launching/session_launch, active/session_execution, retrying/delivery_retry, and exhausted/delivery_terminal. The field remains string-valued for compatible additive server values; current in-repo producer additions must update the authoritative human-code projection and parity tests in the same change. Delivered is not a current diagnosis state.

deliveries.description: Retained canonical lifecycle rows ordered by delivery_created_at DESC then delivery_id DESC.

AgentDeliveryLifecycleRow.description:
Agent-scoped delivery lifecycle row sourced only from canonical
event_deliveries rows joined to the minimum canonical event identity
fields needed for display. This schema is the public row type for
agent.delivery_lifecycle and is distinct from the narrow
AgentDeliveryLifecycle hint embedded in agent.diagnose. It must not
contain dead-letter record details, failure aggregates, retry-policy
recommendations, route-target interpretation, status hints, dashboard
projections, or trace/event rendering.

delivery_delivered_at.description: Canonical event_deliveries.delivered_at when the delivery was settled.

delivery_started_at.description: Canonical event_deliveries.started_at when handler execution has started.

failure.description: Canonical event_deliveries.failure envelope. No read surface may reconstruct it from reason_code or prose.

reason_code.description: Canonical event_deliveries.reason_code summary. Rich terminal evidence remains owned by agent.delivery_diagnostics.

retry_count.description: Canonical event_deliveries.retry_count; the API must not add one or infer retries from receipts.

run_id.description: Canonical delivery/event run identity, read as COALESCE(event_deliveries.run_id, events.run_id).

AgentDiagnosisActive.description:
Selected active-session latest-turn refs for agent.diagnose. This
object is present only when the diagnosis owner selects an active
live agent_sessions row for session/session_per_entity modes and that
selected session has a latest agent_turns row. It is persisted
selected-turn evidence, not proof of actual in-flight work. turn_id is
the selected latest turn. task_id is the same selected latest
agent_turns.task_id and is omitted when empty. entity_id is the same
selected latest agent_turns.entity_id dispatch entity and is omitted
when absent. The owner must not fall back to AgentConfig
EffectiveEntityID(), task target entity, role-scoped current-entity
tool context, dashboard projections, conversation API composition, or
stale/inactive session rows.

AgentDiagnosisLastToolOutcome.description:
Compact selected-turn last-tool evidence for agent.diagnose. This
object is present only when the diagnosis owner selects an active
live agent_sessions row for session/session_per_entity modes, that
selected session has a latest agent_turns row, and that turn has a
canonical turn_summary.tool_results entry. turn_id is the same
selected latest turn used by AgentDiagnosisActive. tool_name,
optional tool_use_id, and optional result come from the last
canonical tool_results entry. ok is the selected latest
agent_turns.parse_ok value; it is not a per-tool execution status.
The owner must omit last_tool_outcome when no selected active live
session, latest turn, canonical summary, or canonical tool result
exists, and must fail closed on malformed canonical summary or tool
result data. It must not fall back to raw response_payload, raw
transcript text, dashboard projections, conversation API composition,
runtime executor telemetry, event/dead-letter data, stale/inactive
sessions, or richer per-tool status/latency/completion/error
diagnostics.

ok.description: Selected latest agent_turns.parse_ok; not a per-tool execution status.

AgentDiagnosisRuntimeState.description:
Narrow runtime-state container for agent.diagnose watchdog evidence.
The diagnosis owner reads the most-recent active agent_sessions row
for session/session_per_entity modes using the same active-session
selector as LoadOperatorAgentDiagnosis, ordered by updated_at DESC then
created_at DESC then session_id ASC. It ignores inactive or terminated rows, omits
runtime_state when there is no active live session or no watchdog
evidence, and fails closed on malformed runtime_state.watchdog data.
Multiple active sessions use that same deterministic current-session
selector; this schema does not promote a broader agent runtime_state
enum.

AgentDiagnosisWatchdog.description:
Watchdog-backed runtime evidence for agent.diagnose. This object is
the public projection of agent_sessions.runtime_state.watchdog after
DecodeConversationRuntimeStateDescriptor validation; it is not the
full #852 agent runtime_state enum and must not be derived from queue,
delivery lifecycle, dashboard state, active task, last tool, token,
failure, dead-letter, restart, or broad session lifecycle heuristics.

last_output_at.description: Required when state is healthy_long_running; omitted when no output has been observed.

attempts.description: Canonical recorded delivery retry counter from event_deliveries.retry_count; the API must not add one or reinterpret by status.

enqueued_at.description: Same timestamp owner as AgentDiagnosisQueue.oldest_pending_age_seconds; currently events.created_at.

agent_id.description: Original agent subscriber selected for replay.

audit_event_id.description: Persisted synthetic event.replayed audit event identity.

event_id.description: Original persisted event requested for agent-scoped replay.

new_delivery.description: Fresh delivery row created for replay_event_id with source_delivery_id lineage to original_delivery.

original_delivery.description: Original agent delivery row used as replay target authority.

replay_event_id.description: Fresh replay event identity created by the canonical event redelivery owner.

session_scope.description: Present only for scoped agent modes. Omitted for mode=task because task agents have no live runtime session scope.

backend_profile.description: Selected backend profile id at write time.

invocation_type.description: Backend profile id recorded by the spend ledger, such as anthropic, claude_cli, openai_compatible, or openai_responses.

model.description: Concrete model string used for legacy grouping and guardrail cost estimation.

model_alias.description: Authored model alias from the agent contract at write time.

provider.description: Resolved provider identity at write time.

resolved_model.description: Concrete provider model resolved from model_alias for backend_profile at write time.

transport.description: Resolved provider transport at write time.

estimated_cost_usd.description: Guardrail cost estimate from BudgetTracker pricing, not provider billing truth.

AgentUsageWindow.description:
Agent usage aggregate window over spend_ledger.created_at. since is
inclusive when supplied; until is exclusive when supplied. If both
are omitted, the owner aggregates all retained spend_ledger rows for
the agent. This schema intentionally makes no run/session/turn or
last-turn claim.

BlockingLayer.description:
Layer-of-the-system identifier when operational_state is "stalled".
Empty string when state is running or terminal. Source of truth:
ProjectRunOperationalStatus. Current owner emits:
  "" (empty) — state is running or terminal
  "scoring_terminal_outcome" — scoring entities have not reached
    a terminal scoring outcome (shortlisted/marginal/rejected)
  "delivery_lifecycle" — no active deliveries but events exist
Additional values may be added as the operator-state owner gains
more diagnostic semantics. Type stays string (not enum) for
forward compatibility — additive owner extensions must not
require a /v2/ bump.

flow_instance.description: Persisted flow instance/catalog path when the definition is nested under a flow.

cleanup.description: Exact result from the preservation cleanup owner.

containers.description: Exact result from the managed container identity filter/stop owner.

ok.description: True for dry-run or fully completed apply; false when the operation completed with explicit partial failures.

plan.description: Exact result from the bundle-delete plan owner.

content_yaml.description: Persisted bundle config bytes for this catalog row.

parsed_json.description: Persisted parsed projection for the bundle row.

BundleHash.description: Canonical v1 bundle identity. Public input surfaces use this shape for bundle_hash; legacy sha256:<hex> fingerprints are not accepted under the bundle_hash name.

BundleIdentity.description: Server bundle identity returned by health.check. bundle_hash is the active canonical v1 bundle identity when the runtime has a promoted source fact and is the value clients use for create-new-work bundle scope. fingerprint is legacy boot/runtime identity metadata retained for health/check compatibility; workflow_name and workflow_version are human-friendly labels. Server-local filesystem paths are intentionally NOT exposed — they leak deployment internals and aren't meaningful to external Phase-2 consumers.

BundleRef.description: Deprecated legacy bundle reference accepted during the #1001 dual-accept transition. New callers use bundle_hash with BundleHash shape once canonical source facts are available. BundleRef carries the legacy sha256 fingerprint only and cannot be combined with bundle_hash; doing so fails closed with UNSUPPORTED_BUNDLE_HASH before runtime/store mutation.

BundleRegisterDataBlobV1.description: Optional structured raw data files for bundle.register; the server persists canonical projection bytes.

entries.description: Ordered data entries sorted strictly by normalized path.

path.description: Strictly sorted slash-separated path under a discovered flow data directory, such as flows/<flow>/data/..., packages/<package>/flows/<flow>/data/..., or a nested flow package path, with the same normalization and collision rules as BundleRegistrationFile.path.

api_version.description: Envelope version for non-destructive bundle.register materialization.

files.description: Text contract files to materialize under the temporary bundle root. package.yaml is required.

path.description: Slash-separated path relative to the bundle root. Empty, dot, dot-dot, absolute, backslash, NUL, non-NFC, duplicate, and ASCII case-colliding paths are invalid.

text.description: UTF-8 text content for YAML, prompt, policy, and other non-data bundle files.

data_size_bytes.description: Size of persisted data_blob bytes, or 0 when has_data is false.

has_data.description: True when the persisted bundle row carries an auxiliary data_blob.

registered.description: True only when this call inserted a new bundle catalog row; false for duplicate same-content registration.

agent_count.description: Number of definition-only agents projected from the persisted bundle row.

data_size_bytes.description: Size of data_blob in bytes, or 0 when has_data is false.

has_data.description: True when the persisted bundle row carries an auxiliary data_blob.

ConversationActivityCounts.description: Compact owner-derived counts for the closed public activity kinds in one turn.

ConversationForkEntitySnapshot.description: Entity projection reconstructed from source-run entity_mutations at the fork point for forkchat read-only tools.

ConversationForkPointDescriptor.description: Canonical validated fork-point descriptor persisted by the conversation fork lifecycle owner.

ConversationForkPointSelector.description: Selector for the source conversation fork point. Exactly one selector kind is accepted. For turn, turn_id is an opaque canonical full ID and is resolved by exact session plus turn lookup; prefixes and ordinals are not accepted. Event and time use the same coordinate owner.

ConversationForkSandboxPolicy.description: Sandbox policy enforced for forkchat execution. Read tools consume only the fork snapshot; side-effecting tools are stub-recorded and must not mutate live state.

ConversationForkSession.description: Forkchat lifecycle header. `conversation.fork_view` consumes the fork-local transcript owner and returns fork turns when conversation.fork_chat has executed. List responses may still return empty turns for lightweight lifecycle rows.

turns.description: Fork-local transcript turns owned by conversation_fork_turns and excluded from normal agent_turns.

ConversationForkSnapshot.description: Source-at-fork snapshot consumed by conversation.fork_chat sandbox execution.

ConversationForkTurn.description: Fork-local transcript turn. It intentionally is not an agent_turns row and may not have a live trigger event.

ConversationTurnListItem.description: Compact single-row ledger projection. Ordered activity and author-visible text exist only in ConversationTurnDetail.

ordinal.description: Stable 1-based display position in canonical ascending order; never accepted as identity.

Cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor. Encoded by the runtime; clients MUST treat as opaque and pass back unchanged.

DeliveryStatus.description: Canonical delivery lifecycle persisted in event_deliveries.status. pending is queued/not started; in_progress is claimed or executing; delivered is successful settlement; failed is retry-visible failure; dead_letter is terminal failure.

DiagnosisEvidence.description: Generic evidence pointer shape for future diagnosis extensions. Current run.diagnose failure evidence is owner-backed by RunDebugFailureDelivery, which carries concrete event_deliveries/dead_letters facts instead of opaque pointers.

kind.description: e.g., event, entity, turn, mailbox_item, dead_letter.

summary.description: Human-readable one-line description of why this evidence supports the diagnosis.

EntityFull.description:
Entity-native shape: typed fields, gates, accumulator state. This
is the destination shape entity.get returns. v1 implementation
may need to translate from the existing instance-shaped readers
in internal/dashboard/server/server.go (which return flat
WorkflowInstance structs without the typed-fields/gates/
accumulated decomposition). The spec commits to the entity-native
shape; implementations are responsible for providing it. A real
entity-read owner may need to land before entity.* methods can
honor this shape, which is why the entity.* methods are
should_have rather than must_have for v1.

accumulated.description: Accumulator state. Map of accumulator key → JSON value; values may be scalars, objects, or arrays depending on accumulate, data_accumulation, compute, or projection writers.

fields.description: Typed entity fields per the entity contract. Shape depends on entity_type.

gates.description: Gate state. Map of gate_name → boolean.

loops.description: Typed bounded-loop runtime projection. The reserved mutable control bucket is never returned.

source_event_id.description: Persisted causal parent/source event id. Omitted for root or unparented events.

deliveries.description: Canonical persisted delivery rows for the published event; empty when the declared event has no subscribers.

source_event_id.description: Verified parent/source event lineage for this publication when supplied. Omitted means the event is unparented and must not be treated as causal checkpoint proof.

EventPublishTargetRoute.description: Singular receiver route identity accepted by /v1/rpc event.publish when run_id is supplied. The API validates the entity belongs to the selected run and flow_instance before persistence. Selector target forms, fan-out target_set, and payload-derived target identity are not part of this schema.

entity_id.description: Concrete receiver entity id for existing-run event.publish target route identity.

flow_instance.description: Concrete receiver flow instance for existing-run event.publish target route identity.

audit_event_id.description: Persisted synthetic event.replayed audit event identity.

event_id.description: Original persisted event requested for replay.

new_deliveries.description: Fresh delivery rows created for replay_event_id. Each row carries source_delivery_id lineage to the original delivery.

original_deliveries.description: Original agent delivery rows used as replay target authority.

replay_event_id.description: Fresh replay event identity. Its source_event_id/parent lineage points at event_id.

downstream_event_id.description: Present when the decision triggered a required event emission.

downstream_event_name.description: Present with downstream_event_id when the decision triggered an event emission.

downstream_subscriber_source.description: Present with downstream_event_id and uses the same vocabulary as MailboxDownstreamPreview.subscriber_source.

downstream_subscribers.description: Present with downstream_event_id; empty array is valid when no subscribers are known.

idempotency_replayed.description: False for first execution; true when returned from the API idempotency replay path.

event_name.description: Terminal decision event name when a downstream route exists.

OpaqueId.description: Opaque identifier. Currently UUID-shaped for runtime-assigned IDs; clients MUST NOT assume UUID shape.

OperationalState.description:
Operational state of a run. Source of truth:
internal/store/run_operational_status_read_surface.go
ProjectRunOperationalStatus. Values are RunTableStatus values
lowercased plus "stalled" as the projection's special
"running-but-not-progressing" state.

result.description: Per-method notification payload. See each subscribe method's notification_schema field.

RunDebugFailureDelivery.description: Concrete failed or terminal delivery evidence for run.diagnose. Source of truth is event_deliveries joined to the owning event; dead_letters is event-scoped terminal evidence when present. Consumers MUST NOT infer this shape from aggregate delivery counts.

dead_letters.description: Event-scoped terminal dead-letter records linked to this failed or dead-letter delivery when present.

retry_eligible.description: True exactly when status is failed, the retry-visible failure state.

terminal.description: True exactly when status is delivered or dead_letter.

RunDiagnosis.description: Run header plus the canonical operator-state projection and concrete failed-delivery evidence. Source of truth: ProjectRunOperationalStatus for operational_state/blocking fields, RunDebugReport.FailedDeliveries for event_deliveries/dead_letters failure evidence, and RunDebugReport.TestQuiescence for deterministic test assertion-barrier readiness.

blocking_reason.description:
Free-form reason string when operational_state is "stalled".
Empty when state is running or terminal. Current owner emits:
  "" (empty)
  "terminal_scoring_outcome_missing"
  "no_active_deliveries"
Additional values may be added additively.

failed_deliveries.description: Failed or dead-letter delivery rows with persisted reason/error/retry/dead-letter evidence for this run.

heuristics.description:
Free-form diagnostic notes from the projection. Current owner
emits one entry per detected condition, e.g. "dead letters
exist for this run".

owner.description: Canonical runtime/store owner that executed the selected-contract fork.

control_reason.description: Separate lifecycle-control reason from run_control_state; for cancelled runs this is the terminal reason and never a failure class.

ended_at.description: Omitted while run is active; present once status is terminal (completed/failed/cancelled/forked).

failure.description: Required exactly when status is failed; forbidden for every other status.

forked_from_run_id.description: Present only if status indicates a fork.

RunTestQuiescence.description: Canonical public test-quiescence projection for deterministic scenario assertions. Source of truth: persisted run completion inputs consumed by run_model.lifecycle.completion, exposed through run.diagnose so test runners do not infer readiness from partial trace rows or sleeps. `ready` is true exactly when all count fields are zero.

active_deliveries.description: Active delivery rows for the run, excluding committed replay-scope marker rows.

active_session_leases.description: Active agent/session leases for the run whose lease has not expired.

due_timers.description: Active timer rows for the run whose fire_at is currently due.

ready.description: True exactly when active_deliveries, unsettled_pipeline_events, due_timers, and active_session_leases are all zero.

unsettled_pipeline_events.description: Same-run non-runtime-log events whose platform/pipeline receipt is absent or not successful.

RunTraceFilter.description: Typed predicates for the canonical composed RunTraceRow owner. Each present field is an OR-list for that field; different fields compose with AND. This schema is consumed by /v1/rpc run.trace and /v1/ws run.subscribe_trace.

RunTraceRow.description:
One joined trace row. Every row is anchored on an event; delivery,
session, and turn fields are sparse and present only where the join
finds a corresponding row. Matches the runtime's canonical
RunDebugTraceRow shape (internal/store/run_debug_read_surface.go).
The runtime owns the join — clients MUST consume rows as-emitted
and MUST NOT reconstruct the join from separate event/delivery/
session/turn lists.

RuntimeIdentityResult.description: Nonsensitive identity for one served runtime process instance. This is the canonical descriptor-trust identity: clients compare runtime_instance_id against local context descriptors after authenticating to the operator API. It is not a bundle fingerprint, PID, port, URL, or unauthenticated process probe.

cleanup.description: Exact result from the run-scoped cleanup/preservation owner.

containers.description: Exact result from the managed entity-container selection/stop owner.

include_bundles.description: Echoes whether this runtime.nuke request includes server-wide bundle catalog deletion; defaults true.

ok.description: True for dry-run or fully completed apply; false when the operation completed with explicit partial failures.

plan.description: Exact result from the destructive-reset plan/result owner.

quiescence.description: Exact result from the active-run/delivery quiescence owner.

ScopeName.description:
Action-resource scopes used for v1 authorization. v1 enforcement
treats every valid token as granting every scope; the enum exists
for forward-compat with Phase 2/3 per-token scope restrictions.
New scopes added by deferred namespaces (e.g., verify:contracts
when verify.* lands, debug:* when debug.* lands) are additive.

Sha256Fingerprint.description: Content-hash fingerprint of a bundle. Stable across paths/worktrees where content is identical.

alias.description: Scenario-local setup alias authored under setup.entities[].as.

flow_instance.description: Concrete flow instance for flow-scoped setup entities. Omitted for root-flow setup rows.

ordinal.description: Stable 1-based display position in canonical ascending order; never accepted as identity.

UsageAccounting.description:
Exact means token counts came from a provider usage object. Estimated
means token counts were locally estimated because the runtime surface
did not expose provider usage. API consumers must not silently merge
exact and estimated buckets without preserving this field.

details.description: Directive execution succeeded and is durable, but settlement/API completion still requires repair. Retry performs finalization only.
The admitted directive execution returned a durable terminal failure. Retrying the operation never executes BoardStep again.
A matching directive operation owns a live execution lease. Retry may observe its durable outcome but must not execute it again.
Directive execution may have produced effects, but no authoritative result was durably recorded. Automatic re-execution is forbidden.

active_sessions.description: Active sessions that prevent implicit target resolution. A session without run_id is reported without a run_id field and still fail-closes implicit resolution.

run_id.description: Present when ambiguity was caused by an explicit run target combined with incompatible legacy targeting flags.

details.description: The requested event is not admitted by the selected event catalog, root-input contract, target, or recipient plan. Root-input reasons preserve the canonical declared and routable domains.

declared_events.description: Deterministically sorted event domain from the owning server validator. For root-input reasons this is the root flow pins.inputs.events declaration.

reason.description: Named server rejection branch. Root-input values are not_declared_root_input and declared_root_input_not_routable.

routable_events.description: Deterministically sorted root inputs with a derived runtime route. Present for root-input reasons, including an empty array when none are routable.
